title: Animal Control

jokes.text: Those are some chairs. That's a cat or opossum.
jokes.setup: Giving a tour of an animal control facility
jokes.punchline: Can't identify basic animals in their workplace
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of incompetence - delivered matter-of-factly which makes it funnier

jokes.text: Oh, this is a napkin where I wrote down a cool name for a dog. 'Bark Obama.' Right?
jokes.setup: Showing off something important in the workplace
jokes.punchline: It's just a terrible dog pun on a napkin
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun but delivered with such pride it becomes funny

jokes.text: We found this bird outside. We tried to turn it into a work whistle, like in the beginning of The Flintstones.
jokes.setup: Explaining what they did with an animal they found
jokes.punchline: They tried to use a live bird as a cartoon whistle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor - treating cartoon logic as real workplace procedure

jokes.text: Thanks!
jokes.setup: Leslie calls them pot-head losers running a miserable suck hole
jokes.punchline: Cheerful, oblivious thank you response
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - one word response that shows complete obliviousness

jokes.text: There's four ways to skin a cat. Regular blade--
jokes.setup: Correcting the idiom 'more than one way to skin a cat'
jokes.punchline: Taking it literally and starting to list actual methods
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that fits character's warped logic perfectly

jokes.text: Coyote trap stepping and reaction
jokes.punchline: Steps in a coyote trap they have lying around
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy setup with good payoff

jokes.text: You're the people you would be calling. That is so trippy.
jokes.setup: Animal control workers want to call someone about vermin problem
jokes.punchline: Realization that they ARE animal control, followed by stoned amazement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of incompetence - they don't understand their own job

jokes.text: This was one of my running feet!
jokes.setup: Leslie caught in trap and in pain
jokes.punchline: Refers to her foot as 'one of my running feet'
jokes.explanation: Leslie's quirky way of speaking - very character-specific

jokes.text: Yo, let's go to Jamaica. No, we should go to Jamaica. Oh, is that what you said? Yeah, dog.
jokes.punchline: They suggest the exact same thing to each other without realizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect stoner logic - shows how out of it they are

jokes.text: Usually, I take it neat, but I will make an exception in the name of health.
jokes.setup: Leslie tells him to drink water
jokes.punchline: Treats water like alcohol - 'neat' vs mixed
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron Swanson - treats everything like alcohol, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Last night, I watched a movie with Diane and the girls in which an orange fish is separated from his father.
jokes.punchline: Refusing to say 'Finding Nemo' - describes it clinically
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron refusing to engage with pop culture on its own terms

jokes.text: Uh, 'Spasm.' No, 'Butter Face'! Very good! Yes!
jokes.setup: Tom giving Andy a scent quiz
jokes.punchline: Terrible cologne names that Andy somehow gets right
jokes.explanation: Absurd cologne names that sound awful but are treated as expertise

jokes.text: The man owns a Rolexus. It's a Lexus filled with Rolexes.
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau word followed by absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - starts with wordplay then gets more ridiculous

jokes.text: I already took what I call a 'dwyer shower.' I rubbed my armpits with air fresheners.
jokes.punchline: Andy's disgusting alternative with made-up name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy gross-out humor with character-specific naming convention

jokes.text: I got the perfect guy. Friend of mine, bit of an alcoholic, a little grabby, either a war hero or war criminal, depending on who you ask, but a good dude.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly alarming qualifications presented as positives
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from minor flaws to major red flags

jokes.text: Gosh, Jeremy, he sounds perfect. Are his gums great too?
jokes.punchline: Leslie's sarcastic response about dental health
jokes.explanation: Leslie's exasperated sarcasm cutting through Jeremy's absurd logic

jokes.text: This whole place runs on dibs.
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex governance to children's claiming system
jokes.explanation: Brilliant encapsulation of government dysfunction in four words

jokes.text: Boring! 'Boring' is my middle name, so...
jokes.punchline: Leslie claiming boring as her middle name
jokes.explanation: Leslie embracing her reputation for thorough bureaucracy

jokes.text: For 'date of birth,' you wrote 'springtime.'
jokes.punchline: Ron's poetic non-answer to basic question
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron Swanson privacy paranoia expressed poetically

jokes.text: One shelf.
jokes.setup: Asked how many drinks per week, says 'one'
jokes.punchline: Clarifies it's one shelf of alcohol, not one drink
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with economic delivery - classic Ron humor

jokes.text: Lovemaking and woodworking.
jokes.punchline: Lists two very different physical activities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron answer - combines his romantic side with his craftsmanship

jokes.text: I have an uncle who does yoga.
jokes.setup: Asked about family history of mental illness
jokes.punchline: Considers yoga a mental illness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ron's extreme masculinity taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Cowardice and weak-willed men. And hazelnuts.
jokes.punchline: Lists character traits as allergies, then adds actual allergy
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation ending with unexpected mundane detail

jokes.text: Epic and private.
jokes.punchline: Two-word summary that says everything and nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - maximum confidence, maximum privacy, maximum economy

jokes.text: If I give as much as I get, I'm giving $20 million. Ay yi yi. That's how much I'm worth, 20...million...dollars.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting charity helps personal brand
jokes.punchline: Dennis completely missing the point and bragging about wealth
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Dennis's narcissism and Tom's discomfort

jokes.text: Ah, he's disgusting, but I want to take his money and give it to needy people, so I'm just gonna keep on smiling. Yay!
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to explain her strategy to camera
jokes.explanation: Good mockumentary moment - Leslie's transparent strategy

jokes.text: Cuban? Now, that's the kind of guy I can hang out with-- Mark Cuban.
jokes.setup: Tom trying to redirect from Bill Gates to Mark Cuban
jokes.punchline: Dennis completely changes attitude based on one name
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's shallow decision-making based on image

jokes.text: I have one testicle-- whac-a-mole accident-- and I'm down to clown.
jokes.setup: Harris introducing himself as job candidate
jokes.punchline: Overshares bizarre injury and uses odd slang
jokes.explanation: Absolutely bonkers introduction that escalates perfectly

jokes.text: Harris, we know who you are. I literally just fired you.
jokes.punchline: Reveal that he was just fired from this same job
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that recontextualizes Harris's enthusiasm

jokes.text: We live together! Yo, we live in the same place! This is my boy! Hey, 'Wyld Stallyns'!
jokes.setup: Two former employees meeting at interview
jokes.punchline: Excited recognition with Bill & Ted reference
jokes.explanation: Shows their stoner friendship dynamic well

jokes.text: Actually, when I went there, it was just a two-year college. Wow, so you're not even technically qualified to work at your current job.
jokes.setup: Candidate saying he went to four-year college
jokes.punchline: Reveals disqualifying detail, Chris points out the logic
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of incompetence with Chris's brutal honesty

jokes.text: I am very, very, very, very frightened of animals.
jokes.punchline: Major disqualifying admission with emphasis
jokes.explanation: Obvious irony but well-delivered with the repetition

jokes.text: You see Dr. Richard Nygard? I'm a Nygardian too!
jokes.punchline: Chris's enthusiastic response with cult-like terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - over-enthusiasm about therapy

jokes.text: The spaying and neutering tools, do you think those fit humans? And is there a lock on the spaying room door?
jokes.setup: Animal-phobic candidate asking about the job
jokes.punchline: Disturbing questions about surgical tools and security
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that takes animal phobia to disturbing logical conclusion

jokes.text: I studied zoology in college, and I can control animals with my mind. Get out. I made you say that.
jokes.punchline: Absurd psychic claim followed by 'proving' it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Orin moment - creepy but with twisted logic

jokes.text: Balsa wood? You could at least use mahogany.
jokes.punchline: Ron critiquing the wood quality
jokes.explanation: Pure Ron - evaluating everything through woodworking expertise

jokes.text: Wish all patients were like that. It's really annoying when they ask questions.
jokes.setup: Ron leaving without hearing test results
jokes.punchline: Doctor's anti-patient sentiment
jokes.explanation: Dark medical humor about patient care

jokes.text: You know, they claim that smoking is bad for you, but is there any medical evidence to back that up? Great point.
jokes.punchline: Doctor agreeing with obviously wrong medical claim
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters' questionable judgment

jokes.text: Our health care outreach program has reduced pink eye in the rural community by 30%... to 70%.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it actually increased the problem
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that subverts expectation of success

jokes.text: We drug them pretty heavily so they can't get very far. In fact, mostly, they just flop around on the ground. Makes it easier to just walk up and 'pow!' Stupid foxes. It's deeply erotic.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing details ending with sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that makes Dennis increasingly repulsive

jokes.text: I think that guy wants to hunt me.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking about Andy's running speed and camouflage
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact realization
jokes.explanation: Andy's understated reaction to obvious threat

jokes.text: You're a dick. Excuse me? How dare you talk to me like that? Where are your manners?
jokes.punchline: Andy calling him out, Dennis claiming moral high ground
jokes.explanation: Good payoff with Andy standing up and Dennis's hypocritical response

jokes.text: I run a children's break dancing studio called 'D.J. Jazzy Vern's.'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous business name for children's activity
jokes.explanation: Absurd business name that captures the character's dated coolness

jokes.text: Hey, you know what, he's not a rack of ribs, so stop grilling him. Nailed it.
jokes.punchline: Jeremy's terrible pun followed by self-satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Weak pun made funny by Jeremy's pride in it

jokes.text: Ribs are better smoked than grilled, so un-nailed it, with the back part of the hammer.
jokes.punchline: Ron's technical correction and metaphor extension
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - correcting both cooking method and metaphor with technical precision

jokes.text: I don't like lotion. I like my hands to be cracked and calloused like a railway worker.
jokes.punchline: April's preference for rough, working hands
jokes.explanation: Perfect April anti-feminine sentiment with specific imagery

jokes.text: I know. I filled the bottles with fake blood, vinegar, and mud.
jokes.punchline: Leslie anticipated this and made gross alternatives
jokes.explanation: Shows Leslie's deep understanding of April's preferences

jokes.text: Batman. See? I failed.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking practice interview question 'What's your name?'
jokes.punchline: April's deliberate wrong answer and matter-of-fact assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sabotaging herself with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: I will be your Pocahontas. Climb into my papoose.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to convince April to do the hearing
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor mixing mentor role with baby carrier
jokes.explanation: Weird Leslie metaphor that makes no logical sense but feels right for her

jokes.text: But only if you divorce Ben. Hey, don't even joke about that. What? I really think he's wrong for you.
jokes.setup: April agreeing to do the hearing
jokes.punchline: Serious condition about Leslie's marriage
jokes.explanation: Shows April's ongoing opposition to Leslie's happiness

jokes.text: I had never heard of several of those terms, and I'm a freak.
jokes.punchline: Admission that they're beyond his experience despite being 'a freak'
jokes.explanation: Jeremy's creepy admission that adds to the discomfort

jokes.text: Alcohol is delicious and fun... damentally a substance that should not be abused.
jokes.punchline: Leslie feeding her the proper answer mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection showing Leslie coaching April

jokes.text: Wow. Ms. Ludgate, are you a ventriloquist? I mean, that is amazing how you make your words come out of Ms. Knope's mouth.
jokes.punchline: Jeremy's sarcastic observation about ventriloquism
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about the obvious coaching

jokes.text: This check is made out to 'Go [bleep] yourself.' In your face! Dennis Feinstein helps no man!
jokes.setup: Dennis seemingly being generous with large donation
jokes.punchline: Fake check with insult, followed by declaration
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reinforces Dennis's horrible character

jokes.text: What's 'cholesterol'?
jokes.punchline: Ron doesn't know what cholesterol is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron ignorance of modern medical concepts

jokes.text: Are you breaking up with me?
jokes.setup: Leslie saying it's not April, it's her about withdrawing application
jokes.punchline: April treating it like a romantic breakup
jokes.explanation: April's romantic interpretation of professional situation

jokes.text: I'm just sick of being on the losing side. I'm going to tell everyone this was my idea. Retro-jammed.
jokes.punchline: Admitting opportunism and making up term 'retro-jammed'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jeremy's opportunistic nature

jokes.text: For the last time, it's a Rolexus, and you can't drive it.
jokes.punchline: Tom correcting the name and adding logic problem
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds more absurd details to the concept

jokes.text: Can I hire an intern? Not yet. Can I hire a Mexican elf? No. Can I buy a child bride? No. Then how is this even a promotion?
jokes.setup: April asking about her new powers
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate requests ending with logical complaint
jokes.explanation: Perfect April escalation with twisted logic at the end

tmdb_overview: Leslie decides to give the Animal Control Department a much-needed makeover. Ann tries to get Ron the medical attention he needs. Ben, Tom, and Andy recruit donors for their charity foundation.
insight: 59 jokes in 27 minutes sustains Leslie's absurdist chaos at 82-point heights.
